name: Somali Region (Ethiopia)

colors.symbolism: the courage and sacrifice of the region's people
the hoist triangle bearing the camel

description: Three horizontal stripes — green, white, and red — with a yellow triangle at the hoist bearing a gold camel beside an acacia tree.
history: The Somali Region (also known as Region 5) is one of the regional states of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia, established under the 1995 Constitution and home to ethnic Somalis in the Ogaden and adjoining areas. The flag's camel symbolises the central role of pastoralism in the region's economy.

features.symbolism: the camel — central to Somali pastoralism — beside an acacia tree
